words.def: a little more or less than; a little before or after
the process of doing something in order to make something happen or to deal with a situation
words.example: The time has come for action if these beautiful animals are to survive.

words.def: a thing that you do for interest or pleasure
a person who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
words.example: Both lead actors (= the ones who play the main parts) are outstanding.

words.def: to put something together with something else so as to increase the size, number, amount, etc.
words.example: add something, Next add the flour.

words.def: feeling fear; frightened because you think that you might be hurt or suffer
the period of time from 12 o'clock in the middle of the day until about 6 o’clock in the evening
one more time; on another occasion
used in expressions of time with the simple past tense to show how far in the past something happened
to have the same opinion as somebody; to say that you have the same opinion
words.example: When he said that, I had to agree.

words.def: a place where planes land and take off and that has buildings for passengers to wait in
in addition; too
words.example: She's fluent in French and German. She also speaks a little Italian.

words.def: at all times; on every occasion
words.example: There's almost always somebody at home in the evenings.

words.def: also; in addition to
having strong feelings about something that you dislike very much or about an unfair situation
words.example: Her behaviour really made me angry.

words.def: a creature that is not a bird, a fish, a reptile, an insect or a human
words.example: the animals and birds of South America

words.def: one more; an extra thing or person
something that you say, write or do to react to a question or situation
words.example: I rang the bell, but there was no answer.

words.def: used instead of someone in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.
used instead of something in negative sentences and in questions; after if/whether; and after verbs such as prevent, ban, avoid, etc.
a round fruit with shiny red or green skin that is fairly hard and white inside
the fourth month of the year, between March and May
words.example: in April, She was born in April.

words.def: to get to a place, especially at the end of a journey
the use of the imagination to express ideas or feelings, particularly in painting, drawing or sculpture
a piece of writing about a particular subject in a newspaper or magazine, on a website, etc.
to say or write something in the form of a question, in order to get information
words.example: How old are you—if you don’t mind me/my asking?

words.def: used to say where something/somebody is or where something happens
words.example: at the corner of the street

words.def: the 8th month of the year, between July and September
the sister of your father or mother; the wife of your uncle or aunt
the season of the year between summer and winter, when leaves change colour and the weather becomes colder
words.example: in the autumn of something, in the autumn of 2010

words.def: to or at a distance from somebody/something in space or time
words.example: The beach is a mile away.

words.def: a very young child or animal
unpleasant; full of problems
words.example: I have some bad news for you, I'm afraid.

words.def: a container made of cloth, leather, plastic or paper, used to carry things in, especially when shopping or travelling
words.example: He was carrying a heavy bag of groceries.

words.def: a round object used for throwing, hitting or kicking in games and sports
a long curved fruit with a thick yellow skin and that is soft inside, which grows on trees in hot countries
a small group of musicians who play popular music together, often with a singer or singers
an organization that provides various financial services, for example keeping or lending money
words.example: I don't have much money in the bank at the end of the month.

words.def: a large, long container that you put water in and then get into to wash your whole body
a room in which there is a bath, a washbasin and often a toilet
words.example: Go and wash your hands in the bathroom.

words.def: used when you are naming people or things, describing them or giving more information about them
an area of sand or small stones (called shingle), next to the sea or a lake
words.example: on the beach, tourists sunbathing on the beach

words.def: having beauty; giving pleasure to the senses or to the mind
for the reason that
words.example: I did it because he told me to.

words.def: to start to be something
words.example: + adj., It soon became apparent that no one was going to come.

words.def: a piece of furniture for sleeping on
a room for sleeping in
to start doing something; to do the first part of something
at or towards the back of somebody/something; further back
words.example: She rode off down the road with the dog running behind.

words.def: to feel certain that something is true or that somebody is telling you the truth
words.example: believe somebody, I don't believe you!

words.def: at or to a lower level, position or place
words.example: They live on the floor below.

words.def: a road vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing the pedals with your feet
words.example: He got on his bicycle and rode off.

words.def: large in size, degree, amount, etc.
a bicycle
words.example: I used to ride my bike around the neighbourhood for hours.

words.def: a creature that is covered with feathers and has two wings and two legs. Most birds can fly.
words.example: a bird’s nest with two eggs in it

words.def: the day in each year which is the same date as the one on which you were born
having the very darkest colour, like coal or the sky at night
having the colour of a clear sky or the sea on a clear day
a vehicle (smaller than a ship) that travels on water, moved by oars, sails or a motor
the whole physical structure of a human or an animal
words.example: The human body is a complex structure.

words.def: not interesting; making you feel tired and impatient
to come out of your mother’s body at the beginning of your life
used with plural nouns to mean ‘the two’ or ‘the one as well as the other’
a glass or plastic container, usually round with straight sides and a narrow neck, used especially for storing liquids
a container made of wood, thick card, metal, etc. with a flat stiff base and sides and often a lid (= cover), used especially for holding solid things
words.example: in a box, Everything we owned was neatly packed in cardboard boxes.

words.def: a male child or a young male person
a man or boy that somebody has a romantic or sexual relationship with
a type of food made from flour, water and usually yeast mixed together and baked
the first meal of the day
words.example: They were having breakfast when I arrived.

words.def: to come to a place with somebody/something
words.example: bring somebody/something (with you), Don't forget to bring your books with you.

words.def: a boy or man who has the same parents as another person
having the colour of earth or coffee
to make something, especially a building, by putting parts together
words.example: build (something), They have permission to build 200 new homes.

words.def: a structure such as a house or school that has a roof and walls
a large road vehicle that carries passengers, especially one that travels along a fixed route and stops regularly to let people get on and off
words.example: by bus, Shall we walk or go by bus?

words.def: the activity of making, buying, selling or supplying goods or services for money
words.example: It's been a pleasure to do business with you.

words.def: having a lot to do; perhaps not free to do something else because you are working on something
a soft yellow food made from cream, used in cooking and for spreading on bread
to obtain something by paying money for it
words.example: buy (something), Where did you buy that dress?

words.def: goodbye
a place where you can buy drinks and simple meals. Alcohol is not usually served in British or American cafes.
words.example: There are small shops and pavement cafes around every corner.

words.def: a sweet food made from a mixture of flour, eggs, butter, sugar, etc. that is baked in an oven. Cakes are made in various shapes and sizes and are often decorated, for example with cream or icing.
the act of speaking to somebody on the phone
words.example: to get/receive a call from somebody

words.def: a piece of equipment for taking photographs, moving pictures or television pictures. It can be a separate item or part of another device.
words.example: Just point the camera and press the button.

words.def: the negative of can; can not
words.example: I cannot believe the price of the tickets!

words.def: a road vehicle with an engine and four wheels that can carry a small number of passengers
words.example: Paula got into the car and drove off.

words.def: thick, stiff paper; a piece of this for writing on
to support the weight of somebody/something and take them or it from place to place; to take somebody/something from one place to another
words.example: carry somebody/something, He was carrying a large bag.

words.def: a small animal with soft fur that people often keep as a pet. Cats catch and kill birds and mice.
a small disc on which sound or information is recorded. CDs can be played or read on various types of machines, including CD players and computers. (the abbreviation for ‘compact disc’)
words.example: on CD, His albums are available on CD and online.

words.def: the act or result of something becoming different
words.example: change in something, We're hoping for a change in the weather.

words.def: a type of food made from milk that can be either soft or hard and is usually white or yellow in colour; a particular type of this food
a large bird that is often kept for its eggs or meat
words.example: They keep chickens in the back yard.

words.def: a young human who is not yet an adult
a hard brown sweet food made from cocoa beans, used in cooking to add taste to cakes, etc. or eaten as a sweet
to decide which thing or person you want out of the ones that are available
a building in which films are shown
a large and important town
words.example: Parking is difficult in the city centre.

words.def: a group of students who are taught together
words.example: in the/your class, We were in the same class at school.

words.def: a room where a class of children or students is taught
not dirty
an instrument for measuring and showing time, in a room, on the wall of a building or on a computer screen (not worn or carried like a watch)
the things that you wear, such as trousers, dresses and jackets
words.example: I bought some new clothes for the trip.

words.def: an organization for people who share an interest or do a sport or activity together
a piece of outdoor clothing that is worn over other clothes to keep warm or dry. Coats have sleeves (= parts covering the arms) and may be long or short.
the roasted seeds (called coffee beans) of a tropical bush; a powder made from them
